steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that greater pressure fluctuations at the snout lead to better avoidance of obstructions in low visibility. To weaken it, a finding must show that higher pressure fluctuations do NOT produce better obstacle avoidance.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — A study using obstructions that created large vortices in low-visibility conditions found that the *Synodontis macropunctata*, which has a relatively wide head, bumped into less than half of the obstructions.: Without a comparison group, one species’ collision rate below 50% does not directly contradict the prediction that higher pressure fluctuations produce better avoidance across head shapes. ✗
  • Choice B — A study using obstructions that created large vortices in low-visibility conditions found that the intermediate-headed dusky smooth-hound (*Mustelus canis*) bumped into the obstructions just as often as the wide-headed *Synodontis macropunctata* did.: Intermediate-headed fish produce higher pressure fluctuations than wide-headed fish, so the hypothesis predicts they should avoid obstructions better. Equal collision rates directly contradict that prediction. ✓
  • Choice C — A study using obstructions that created large vortices in low-visibility conditions found that the wide-headed *Synodontis macropunctata* bumped into obstructions more often than the intermediate-headed dusky smooth-hound (*Mustelus canis*) did.: Wide-headed fish have lower pressure fluctuations than intermediate-headed fish, so their colliding more often confirms the hypothesis rather than weakening it. ✗
  • Choice D — A study using obstructions that created large vortices in low-visibility conditions found that some specimens of the intermediate-headed dusky smooth-hound (*Mustelus canis*) bumped into the obstructions more often than others did.: Individual variation within a group is unrelated to whether head shape and pressure fluctuations predict avoidance across groups. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
